sections.a_eyebrow: Soft Shadow · Neumorphic · Soft · Rounded · Calm
sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Soft UI — combining soft shadow, neumorphic, soft elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value soft shadow and neumorphic design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Soft UI style use case illustration
Soft UI style use case illustration
Soft UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Soft UI — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: light (#e8e0f0). The .inner panel has a clean background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #8b5cf6 as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #2a1a3a for headings, #6a5a7a for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of soft ui through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in Quicksand — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #8b5cf6. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #6a5a7a. Buttons: primary — #8b5cf6 background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #8b5cf6 border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), sans-serif stack.
